chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-1281 of 2025 [Hafiz Ziauddin Sultan v. NED University of Engineering & Technology] ______________________________________________________________ Date Order With Signature Of Judges ______________________________________________________________ For orders as to maintainability 23.10.2025. Petitioner is present in person. Mr. Khalid Javed, advocate for respondent. ------------ MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO,J:- We have heard the parties. Learned counsel for respondent University has drawn our attention to Regulations 4.1 and 4.3(b) to differentiate the formulas applied for giving grades to a candidate. According to Regulation 4.3(b), 40% of total marks obtained in third year and 60% of total marks obtained in fourth year are aggregated to decide grading. In the case of petitioner, on such formula, the aggregate percentage is 53.89% in Second Division. Petitioner submits that his score in B. Tech exams shall also be included for calculating his percentage and Division. However, this is not provided in the Regulations of the University and, therefore, petitioner, having gone through the relevant Regulations is satisfied that he has no case, and this petition is accordingly disposed of with consent. The petition is accordingly disposed of in above terms. JUDGE JUDGE HANIF
